rulings.Case Title: DAVID SKOKAN et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO TAXI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 20, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANTS HOANG KIM, LOC TRAN's Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Loc Tran's unopposed motion for a protective order is granted. Defendant's deposition shall be taken at his counsel's offices in San Francisco, California. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KELLY O'HAIRE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order That The Deposition Of The Mayor Of San Francisco Not Be Taken
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 20, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Protective Order That The Deposition Of The Mayor Of San Francisco Not Be Taken. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant City and Count of SF's motion for Protective Order is granted. Plaintiff has failed to make a "clear showing" that Mayor Lee has "direct personal factual information pertaining to material issues" in this litigation." See Nagle v. Sup. Ct. (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 1465, 1467-68; Liberty Mut. Ins. Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1282, 1289. Mayor Lee has already presented sworn responses to plaintiff in exchange for withdrawing the Notice of Deposition and he does not recall the conversation he had with District Attorney Gascon. Furthermore, even if he did remember, it is not clear how his testimony about this conversation would be relevant to plaintiff's claims, much less be material to her case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KELLY O'HAIRE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Seal Confidential Portions Of Its Motion For Protective Order That The Deposition Of The Mayor Of San Francisco Not Be Taken
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 20, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Seal Confidential Portions Of Its Motion For Protective Order That The Deposition Of The Mayor Of San Francisco Not Be Taken. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion to Seal is denied. Rules 2.550 and 2.551 of California Rules of Court do not permit the sealing of records in discovery motions. In fact, discovery motions are specifically excluded from the Rules. See Rule 2.550(a)(3). The parties have a protective order in place that would allow for these documents to be confidential and for the parties to designate the documents as confidential. Therefore, the records are confidential pursuant to the protective order and an order to seal is neither permitted nor necessary. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 20, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANTS HAYLEE YEE, SUZANNE YEE Motion To Compel Plaintiff Genesio Lobato's Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two, Plaintiff Thomas Mcvitty's Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And Plaintiffs Phillip Collins', Genesio Lobato's, Thomas Mcvitty's, And Gary Cheatle's Production Of Documents In Response To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Defendants' motion to compel further responses to their Requests for Production of Documents, Set 1, No. 12 is granted. Plaintiffs shall verify the subsequent responses that they provided on March 9, 2015. Defendants' motion to compel further responses from Plaintiff Lobato to their Special Interrogatories, Set 2, Nos. 120-122, 125-128, 133, 139, 142-145, 148-150 is moot in lights of Plaintiff's subsequent further responses. Defendants' motion to compel further responses from Plaintiff Lobato to their Special Interrogatories, Set 2, Nos. 151-155 is granted and Nos. 156-158 have been withdrawn. The information sought is relevant to Plaintiff's claim for damages and considerations of Plaintiff's privacy do not outweigh the relevance of the information to Defendants' evaluation of any appropriate damages. (Continued to Part 2 of 2.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PHILLIP COLLINS et al VS. HAYLEE YEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Haylee Yee, Suzanne Yee Motion To Compel Plaintiff Genesio Lobato'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two, Plaintiff Thomas Mcvitty'S Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And Plaintiffs Phillip Collins', Genesio Lobato'S, Thomas Mcvitty'S, And Gary Cheatle'S Production Of Documents In Response To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions (Duplicate Entry - For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 20, 2015, Line 3, Defendants Haylee Yee, Suzanne Yee Motion To Compel Plaintiff Genesio Lobato's Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two, Plaintiff Thomas Mcvitty's Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And Plaintiffs Phillip Collins', Genesio Lobato's, Thomas Mcvitty's, And Gary Cheatle's Production Of Documents In Response To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions. (Duplicate Entry - For Tentative Ruling Only) (Part 2 of 2.) Defendants' motion to compel further responses from Plaintiff McVitty to their Requests for Production of Documents, Set 2, No. 46 is granted and No. 47 is withdrawn. The information sought is relevant to Plaintiff's claim for damages and considerations of Plaintiff's privacy and his boilerplate objections do not outweigh the relevance of the information to Defendants' evaluation of any appropriate damages. Defendants' motion for sanctions is granted. Plaintiffs and their counsel are sanctioned for misuse of the discovery process because their objections were without substantial justification and they failed to engage in a meaningful meet-and-confer process. Plaintiffs and their counsel are jointly and severally sanctioned in the amount of $1,315.50, payable to Defendants no later than April 20, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LYNNE CRAWFORD VS. ROBERT J. LIS, AN INDIVIDUAL DOING BUSINESS AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Deft Robert Lis Motion To Compel Compliance With Contractual Dispute Resolution Provisions, And Motion To Stay Pending Litigation.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 20, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT BRIAN KAUFMAN Joinder In Deft Robert Lis Motion To Compel Compliance With Contractual Dispute Resolution Provisions, And Motion To Stay Pending Litigation. Motion to Compel Arbitration is denied. Under CCP § 1281.2, a court may deny a motion to compel arbitration when a party to the arbitration agreement is also a party to a pending court action with a third party that arises out of the same transaction or series of related transactions and presents a possibility of conflicting rulings on a common issue of law or fact. Henry v. Alcove Inv., Inc., (1991) 233 Cal. App. 3d 94. Plaintiff has not established that Defendant has waived his right to compel arbitration. However, because allegations for negligence against all Defendants stem from the same series of events and Defendant Kaufman is not subject to binding arbitration the court denies the motion to compel arbitration. Balancing the interests of Plaintiff, Defendant Robert J. Lis, and Defendant Tanglefoot Master Builders, who have signed a contractual arbitration agreement, with Brian Kaufman, who has not signed a contractual arbitration agreement, the court finds in favor of denying the petition to compel arbitration and stay pending litigation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KIMBERLY LANDIS et al VS. HELEN OF TROY LIMITED CLARENDON HOUSE, DBA OXO et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 20, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT OXO INTERNATIONAL LTD. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The motion is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant acted with a conscious disregard to the safety of others. Plaintiff's evidence shows that between February of 2010 and June of 2012 (prior to plaintiff's accident) there were 12 similar exploding incidents. Defendant's person most knowledgeable (Mr. Cutsuries) admitted at pgs. 52-53 of his deposition that there was an "uptick" in the exploding incidents around the time plaintiff was hurt. A trier of fact could find that defendant should have done more to insure the safety of the public and its failure to do so was malicious. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From One Medical Group; To Compel Joy B. To Authorize The Release Of The One Medical Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 20, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT ANDREW S.' Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From One Medical Group; To Compel Joy B. To Authorize The Release Of The One Medical Documents And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's request for an injunction requiring Plaintiff to sign a HIPAA release is denied. There is no legal basis or authority for requiring such an order. Both parties remain free to voluntarily sign such a release. Defendant's motion to compel production of his subpoena to One Medical Group is denied. The subpoena did not provide the required 15-day notice before production was due and, on that basis alone, cannot be enforced. Rosenfeld v. Abraham Joshua Heschel Day School, Inc. (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 886, 903-904. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Andrew S. To Complete Discovery Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 20, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT ANDREW S.' Motion To Complete Discovery Proceedings. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to extend the discovery cut-off date is denied. Extending the deadline would prejudice Plaintiff's ability to prepare for trial and would allow Defendant to improperly surprise Plaintiff at trial with evidence that was gathered literally in the days leading up to the trial. This would not allow Plaintiff the time she needs to prepare her case and respond to whatever evidence is gathered by Defendant. Furthermore, waiting to notice these depositions until days before the discovery cut-off exposed Defendant to the risk that they would be delayed. These were out-of-state depositions of non-parties that will require them to provide detailed, private, and intimate details about their personal lives. Accordingly, objections and delays should have been expected and planned into the schedule. The parties note that a motion to continue the trial date is pending before this Court. Should that motion be granted, it will allow Defendant to gather this additional evidence without prejudicing Plaintiff. Until that motion is resolved, however, it would be improper to extend the discovery cut-off. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HERMANPREET SINGH VS. MERIDIAN BUILDERS & DEVELOPERS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 20, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT HOMEAWAY, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled in part and sustained in part. As to the first cause of action for premises liability, the demurrer is overruled. The complaint adequately alleges the elements of premises liability. (Complaint, pars. 8, 15-16.) As to the second cause of action for strict product liability, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The complaint does not allege that Defendant manufactured or marketed any "tangible personal property." (Restatement 3d, Torts: Product Liability sec. 19.) Further, Peterson v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 10 Cal.4th 1185, 1190, provides that a cause of action for strict product liability cannot be maintained under these circumstances, as it would be an "unwarranted extension of the doctrine of products liability." As to the third cause of action for breach of warranty, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The complaint does not allege that Plaintiff was in contractual privity with Defendant. "Privity of contract is a prerequisite in California for recovery on a theory of breach of implied warranties of fitness and merchantability." (All West Electronics, Inc. v. M-B-W, Inc. (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 717, 725.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA GUERRERO et al VS. DIGNITY HEALTH INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 20, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT STUART LINNE Motion To Change Venue. Hearing Required. =(302/EHG)
